title: helictotrichon pratense subsp. sulcatum
intro: Helictotrichon pratense subsp. sulcatum, commonly known as sulcate oat grass, is a type of perennial grass belonging to the family Poaceae. It is native to Europe and Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of sulcate oat grass include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
